examples: We're like ants in a large anthill and we carry out our tasks, even when we don't want to.
Sometimes culture resembles "one big stomped anthill," wrote John P. Robinson and Geoffrey Godbey in their book Time for Life.
Instead of feeling happiness and awe from staring at an anthill and watching the mysteries of life unfold, you decided to forget those small pleasures.
Few pieces have provoked such a furious response, at least within the tiny anthill that is the Brussels Euro-bubble.
